_comment: Skoll sandbox seccomp profile. Layered hardening ON TOP OF the runtime boundary (gVisor 'runsc' in prod, runc in dev). Default action is ALLOW; we explicitly ERRNO the syscall families an agent shell never legitimately needs and that are the usual ingredients of container-escape / privilege-escalation chains. Under runsc the gVisor user-space kernel is the primary boundary and most of these are not even reachable; under runc this profile is meaningful defence in depth. Pass to docker via --security-opt seccomp=sandbox/seccomp.json. NOTE: do NOT add a profile narrower than this without testing build-essential / git / pip, which need a broad syscall surface.

syscalls.comment: Module loading, kernel tuning, mount manipulation, time/clock changes, and other CAP_SYS_ADMIN-class operations. An agent shell never needs these; they are common escape primitives.

Joining an existing namespace. Never needed by an agent shell. NOTE: we intentionally do NOT block clone/unshare here — clone() with SIGCHLD is how glibc implements fork(), so an unconditional block would break bash/git/pip entirely. Namespace *creation* is instead prevented by --cap-drop=ALL (CLONE_NEWUSER/NEWNS/NEWNET require CAP_SYS_ADMIN, which we drop), which is the correct and robust control.
Cross-process memory inspection / debugging. Not needed for build+test; denies a class of in-container snooping and breakout-assist techniques.
